conj.statement: ** Pre-Kantian rationalism/empiricism (c. 1600–1780) emerged *exclusively* in Europe (Descartes, Spinoza, Locke, Hume); colonial North America transmitted ideas but produced no original systematic philosophy pre-1776. 2. **Jurisdictional Fragmentation:** 13 British colonies operated under distinct charters (royal, proprietary, corporate), with no unified "American" governance before 1774 (First Continental Congress). 3. **Demographic Reality:** Pre-1700 European settler populations were tiny (e.g., ~5,000 in all of New England in 1640), making "policy analysis" an anachronistic framing—colonial administration focused on local magistrates, not national policy.
conj.subtopic: Explicitly state the anachronism upfront—no sugarcoating
